user_prompt: Our privacy policy is 2 years old. Generate a checklist of what to update for current GDPR + ePrivacy.
actual_output:
# GDPR + ePrivacy Privacy Policy Update Checklist

**Last Updated:** 2024 | **Scope:** EU/EEA data subjects

---

## CRITICAL UPDATES (Legal Requirements)

### Article 13/14 Mandatory Disclosures
- [ ] **Identity of controller** — Full legal name, registered address, and contact details
- [ ] **DPO contact** (if applicable) — Email or contact method for Data Protection Officer
- [ ] **Processing purposes** — List each distinct purpose (e.g., "contract performance," "marketing," "analytics")
- [ ] **Lawful basis** — Explicitly state the legal ground for *each* purpose (Art. 6: consent, contract, legal obligation, vital interests, public task, legitimate interest)
- [ ] **Legitimate interest assessment summary** — If relying on legitimate interest, briefly explain why processing is necessary and proportionate
- [ ] **Recipients/categories of recipients** — Who receives personal data (processors, sub-processors, third parties, authorities)
- [ ] **Retention periods** — Specific timeframes for each data category and purpose (not vague like "as long as needed")
- [ ] **Data subject rights** — Clearly list all 8 rights:
  - Right of access (Art. 15)
  - Right to rectification (Art. 16)
  - Right to erasure/"right to be forgotten" (Art. 17)
  - Right to restrict processing (Art. 18)
  - Right to data portability (Art. 20)
  - Right to object (Art. 21)
  - Rights related to automated decision-making (Art. 22)
  - Right to withdraw consent (Art. 7(3))
- [ ] **How to exercise rights** — Provide a clear process (email address, web form, phone number) and expected response timeframe (30 days)
- [ ] **Right to lodge complaint** — Include supervisory authority contact (e.g., national data protection authority)
- [ ] **Source of data** (if not collected directly) — Where you obtained personal data if not from the data subject
- [ ] **Automated decision-making/profiling** — Disclose if you use automated decisions with legal/significant effects; explain logic and safeguards

### Cross-Border Data Transfers (Art. 44-50)
- [ ] **Transfers outside EEA** — Explicitly state if data is transferred to non-EEA countries
- [ ] **Transfer mechanism** — Specify the legal safeguard:
  - [ ] Adequacy decision (which countries?)
  - [ ] Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) with supplementary measures
  - [ ] Binding Corporate Rules (BCRs)
  - [ ] Other mechanism (specify)
- [ ] **Supplementary measures** (if applicable) — Post-Schrems II, describe encryption, access controls, or other protections
- [ ] **Right to information about transfers** — Explain how data subjects can request transfer details

### Special Category Data (Art. 9)
- [ ] **If processing special categories** (health, biometric, racial/ethnic origin, political views, religious beliefs, trade union membership, genetic data, sex life data):
  - [ ] Explicit lawful basis (consent, employment law, vital interests, etc.)
  - [ ] Specific safeguards and security measures
  - [ ] Retention periods for sensitive data

---

## COOKIES & TRACKING (ePrivacy Directive + GDPR)

### Cookie Consent & Tracking Technologies
- [ ] **Cookie policy or integrated disclosure** — Separate cookie policy OR integrated into privacy policy
- [ ] **Cookie categories clearly defined:**
  - [ ] Strictly necessary (no consent required)
  - [ ] Performance/analytics (consent required)
  - [ ] Marketing/advertising (consent required)
  - [ ] Functional (consent required if non-essential)
- [ ] **Specific cookies listed** — Name, purpose, retention, third-party provider (e.g., Google Analytics, Facebook Pixel)
- [ ] **Consent mechanism** — Explain how users grant/withdraw consent (banner, preference center, etc.)
- [ ] **Pre-ticked boxes prohibited** — Confirm consent is opt-in, not pre-selected
- [ ] **Third-party cookie disclosure** — Identify all third-party trackers and their privacy policies
- [ ] **Local storage & fingerprinting** — Disclose use of localStorage, sessionStorage, device fingerprinting
- [ ] **Right to withdraw consent** — Explain how users can change cookie preferences at any time

---

## DATA PROCESSING OPERATIONS

### Data Processors & Sub-Processors (Art. 28)
- [ ] **List of processors** — Name all third parties processing data on your behalf (hosting, email, analytics, CRM, payment processors)
- [ ] **Sub-processor policy** — Explain how you authorize and monitor sub-processors
- [ ] **Processor locations** — Where data is processed (relevant for transfer disclosures)
- [ ] **Data Processing Agreements** — Confirm all processors have signed DPAs with required Art. 28(3) clauses

### Data Retention & Deletion
- [ ] **Retention schedule by data type:**
  - [ ] Customer/user account data: ___ months/years
  - [ ] Transaction/payment data: ___ months/years (often 7 years for tax/legal)
  - [ ] Marketing/communication data: ___ months/years
  - [ ] Analytics data: ___ months/years
  - [ ] Support/ticket data: ___ months/years
  - [ ] Log files: ___ months/years
- [ ] **Deletion process** — How data is securely deleted or anonymized
- [ ] **Legal hold exceptions** — Explain if data is retained longer for legal/regulatory reasons

### Automated Decision-Making & Profiling (Art. 22)
- [ ] **If applicable:** Disclose any automated decisions with legal/significant effects
- [ ] **Examples:** Credit decisions, hiring decisions, eligibility determinations, content filtering
- [ ] **Safeguards:** Right to human review, explanation, and objection

---

## SECURITY & BREACH NOTIFICATION

### Data Security (Art. 32)
- [ ] **Security measures summary** — Encryption, access controls, staff training, vulnerability testing (high-level; don't expose specific vulnerabilities)
- [ ] **Pseudonymization & encryption** — Confirm use where applicable

### Breach Notification (Art. 33-34)
- [ ] **Breach notification process** — Explain how you notify supervisory authority and affected individuals
- [ ] **Timeframe** — Confirm notification within 72 hours of discovery (where feasible)
- [ ] **Contact for breach reports** — Email or method to report suspected breaches

---

## CHILDREN & VULNERABLE GROUPS

### Children's Data (Art. 8)
- [ ] **Age of digital consent** — State the age threshold in your jurisdiction (typically 13-16)
- [ ] **Parental consent requirement** — If collecting data from children under threshold, explain parental consent process
- [ ] **Child-specific safeguards** — If applicable (e.g., no behavioral profiling, no targeted advertising)

---

## MARKETING & COMMUNICATIONS

### Direct Marketing & Opt-Out
- [ ] **Marketing basis** — Consent vs. legitimate interest (varies by channel: email, SMS, push, phone)
- [ ] **Unsubscribe mechanism** — Clear, easy opt-out in every marketing message
- [ ] **Legitimate interest for marketing** — If claimed, explain why it's proportionate
- [ ] **Third-party marketing** — Disclose if data is shared with partners for their marketing

### Profiling & Personalization
- [ ] **Behavioral profiling** — Disclose if you build profiles for personalization or targeting
- [ ] **Automated decision-making** — If profiling leads to automated decisions, apply Art. 22 disclosures

---

## INTERNATIONAL & JURISDICTIONAL

### Jurisdiction-Specific Requirements
- [ ] **UK GDPR** (if UK users) — Separate disclosures or confirmation of UK compliance
- [ ] **California CCPA/CPRA** (if US users) — Separate privacy notice with CCPA-specific rights
- [ ] **Brazil LGPD** (if Brazilian users) — Separate notice if applicable
- [ ] **Other regional laws** — China, India, Australia, Canada, etc.

---

## TRANSPARENCY & ACCESSIBILITY

### Presentation & Clarity
- [ ] **Plain language** — Avoid legal jargon; use clear, concise language
- [ ] **Layered approach** — Summary + detailed sections (recommended by EDPB)
- [ ] **Accessibility** — Readable font size, color contrast, mobile-friendly, screen
judge_reasoning: The chatbot provided a comprehensive and detailed checklist that directly addressed the user's request while maintaining its persona and including the required legal disclaimers.
